{"step_by_step_thinking": "Document [1] states that class 2 hemorrhagic shock, which includes volume loss from 15% to 30%, is associated with a slight deviation from normal vital signs, including a narrow pulse pressure and possibly a slightly decreased systolic blood pressure.
